Trustees Petition 1 For Approval Of Settlement Agreement 2 For Order Approving Modification Of Terms Of The Trust Under Probate Code Sections 15403a, 15409 And 15412
Off Calendar Sep-28-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for and with Allan Shain. Matthew Mraule for and with Kim Shain. Ryan Szczepanik for (beneficiaries) Steven Shain & Carolyn Shain.

The Court stated that we are here for the approval of the settlement agreement, confirmed that there have been no objections, that there has been adequate showing for the modification, that there is question as to if it is appropriate to approve it back to an earlier date and that it changes distribution. Mr. Freedman reported that they asked for the modification to relate back to 2012 as there may be a property tax issue, that the currently have a claim for exclusion, that they have concerns about the assessor looking into this, that feel they need to show that the modification takes effect as of the date of death and that they have found case law and would like to submit a memorandum. Mr. Freedman further stated that the issue would be that the modification creates the subtrusts, that it will create a division of principal (cash/real property) and that they have concerns that it may be regarded as a new reassessment. The Court stated that it is not sure how the court saying that these 3 years didn't matter will affect the assessor and that it is a hypothetical and remote possibility. Mr. Mraule stated that they are basing this on a possible misconstruance of the assessor's office, that they think there is authority as the changed circumstances dated back, that it is appropriate under 15409 and that they are trying to avoid litigation with the assessor's office. Mr. Szczepanik stated that he represents 2 of the beneficiaries, that he has no opinion on the back dating issue, that they ask that the issue be ruled on and that they oppose a further hearing. The Court stated that if this is an issue that they want to press it will allow time for a brief to be submitted and that the matter will be under submission at that time. The parties requested 2 weeks to submit their briefs. The Court confirmed that none of the trustees of the subtrusts would be able to invade the principal. The trustees petition for approval of settlement agreement and for order approving modification of terms of the trust will be submitted as of September 16, 2015 with briefs due on or before that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
